records.draft_before: OpenClaw's real pitch isn't privacy. It's control. Running agents on hardware you own means the API costs stop when you stop. That's the thing cloud-hosted AI can't match.
records.lesson: Avoid 'real X isn't A. It's B.' structures. Start with the concrete operational detail instead.
records.replacement_pattern: Running agents on hardware you own has one very boring advantage: the API costs stop when you stop.

records.draft_before: The real operator tooling market isn't dashboards. It's the invisible stuff: trace visualization, latency attribution, and context injection when an agent veers off-script.
records.lesson: Do not announce the insight with a tidy antithesis. Lead with the concrete mechanisms.
records.replacement_pattern: Trace visualization, latency attribution, and context injection are the boring parts that decide whether an agent survives contact with production.

records.draft_before: That gets messy when data is fragmented across platforms.
records.lesson: Avoid 'That gets messy when...' and other generic connective phrases. Prefer concrete examples first, then the spoken-cadence turn.
records.replacement_pattern: Things like WhatsApp history, tweet archives, or bookmarks you might need years from now, that's where it gets messy.

records.draft_before: Solo builders should treat the prompt as a work order, not a motivational speech.
records.angel_feedback: avoid tidy antithesis except as a last resort; prefer direct colon framing
records.lesson: Use colon framing when it names concrete parts of the work. Avoid polished antithesis that sounds copywritten.
records.replacement_pattern: Solo builders should treat the prompt as a work order: Name the files, name the constraints, name the stop condition.

records.draft_before: The disable setting gets a polished coauthor badge.
records.angel_feedback: Prefer concrete, plainspoken labels like 'I helped' over polished alternatives like 'I participated.'
records.lesson: When mocking product theater, make the joke dumb-on-purpose and keep the target visible.
records.replacement_pattern: The disable setting gets a little plastic badge that says 'I helped.'
